title: Novae Linguae Certification (v0.2)
description: A signed certification record: a certifier's attestation that a function record passed every 'verified by default' check (typecheck, effects, refinement, termination, complexity/cost). Produced by `nl-validator certify --sign <seed>`. Hashed and signed by exactly the same canonical-serialization rules as a Nova Locutio message (remove 'hash' and 'signature' before hashing; remove only 'signature' before signing; resolve the signer's key from the 'from' DID), differing only in the address prefix: a certification 'hash' is rendered as `cert_<64 hex>`. It is a content-addressed, tamper-evident commons artifact other agents can rely on when assembling.

kind.description: Discriminates this artifact as a certification (vs a function record or a Nova Locutio message).

subject.description: Content-address of the function record this certification attests to.

body_hash.description: Content-address of the body expression that was checked against the record's declared metadata.

checks.description: Per-check verdicts. Each entry names the check, its verdict, and a human detail.

certified.description: True unless a check actively FAILED its declaration (an ILL-TYPED body, an UNDER-DECLARED effect, or a VIOLATED refinement). Conservative UNVERIFIABLE verdicts do not revoke certification.

timestamp.description: Optional ISO-8601 time the certification was produced. Omitted for a byte-reproducible certificate.

from.description: DID of the certifying agent (the Ed25519 public key that signed this certification).

hash.description: BLAKE3-256 of the canonical serialization of this certification with 'hash' and 'signature' removed.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature over the canonical serialization with only 'signature' removed (so the signature covers the hash).
